heading: Introduction
text: SequiaViz open-source platform patient-centric integration visualization multi-omics data clinical oncology diagnostics. combines results variant calling, fusion detection, expression profiling embedded genome browsing, dynamic pathway visualization, automated clinical reporting—within secure, locally deployable framework.

heading: Key features
text: Multi-Omics Integration: Unified interface variants, fusions, expression data multi-site comparisonsInteractive Variant Analysis: Filterable tables, Variant Allele Frequency (VAF) Sankey visualizationFusion Gene Validation: IGV snapshots supporting evidence confidence metricsEmbedded Genome Browsing: Integrated IGV viewer read-level validation without leaving applicationDynamic Pathway Visualization: Interactive networks using Cytoscape STRING DB KEGG pathway integrationExpression Analysis: Tissue-matched reference data volcano plots deregulated genesClinical Reporting: Automated draft generation predefined custom templatesSession Management: Save load analysis sessions reproducible workflowsSecure Deployment: Docker containerization local, data-secure infrastructureUser-Friendly Interface: Guided workflow, real-time tables, downloadable results

heading: Live demo
text:
hosting live demo SequiaViz showcase capabilities allow users explore platform action.
Access demo : Live DemoImportant Notes:Demo data anonymized represent real clinical casesSome features may limited functionality due data sensitivity requirementsSession data persisted visitsWhile demo runs full version SequiaViz, uploading data supported. strongly discourage uploading sensitive personal data demo environment. production use data, deploy SequiaViz locally using Docker.production use sensitive data, deploy SequiaViz locally using Docker

heading: How to install and run
text: SequiaViz deployed using Docker containers easy local deployment. setup consists two interconnected services managed Docker Compose.

heading: Prerequisites
text: SequiaViz currently supports Linux . can run SequiaViz locally, ensure following installed:

heading: 1. Docker Installation
text: Linux: Install Docker using package manager (e.g., sudo apt-get install docker.io Ubuntu).Verify installation running:

heading: 2. Docker Compose
text: Docker Compose comes bundled Docker Desktop. Verify ’s installed:

heading: 3. System Requirements
text: Disk Space: least 10 GB free space Docker images dataRAM: Minimum 4 GB, recommended 8 GB moreCPU: Multi-core processor recommendedInternet Connection: Required pull Docker images STRING DB API access (needed dynamic pathway visualization)

heading: 4. Input Files
text: Prepare data files input_files directory project folder. details required file formats specifications, see Data Requirements chapter.

command pulls required images starts SequiaViz application IGV static server background.Start Application Docker Compose:

heading: Troubleshooting
text:
Container fails start:Input files accessible:
Ensure input_files directory exists contains data files. Check file permissions.Slow performance:
Increase Docker’s allocated CPU RAM Docker Desktop settings (Preferences → Resources).
code:
docker-compose logs sequiaviz-shiny
docker-compose logs igv-static

heading: Stopping and Cleaning Up
text: Stop application:Remove containers images:
code:
docker-compose downdocker-compose down -v
docker image prune -a

heading: Notes
text: input_files directory mounted read-IGV server read-write SequiaVizBoth services communicate via internal Docker network — manual network configuration neededSession data stored within containers; use Docker volumes persistence neededAll data remains local machine; nothing sent external serversFor organizational deployment: ’re interested using SequiaViz don’t Docker expertise, contact bioinformatics team department. can help set maintain SequiaViz organization’s infrastructure.

heading: Step 2: Validation and confirmation
text:
application searches selected directory identifies relevant files.displays overview files found patient selected dataset.marks problems using colored icons:
🟢 Green: required files found (e.g., variant file, fusion file, expression file).
🟠 Orange: Optional files missing (e.g., BAM files IGV visualization). can continue without files, features unavailable.
🔴 Red: Required files missing many files. must resolve issues proceeding. See Common Errors Solutions section help.
🟢 Green: required files found (e.g., variant file, fusion file, expression file).🟠 Orange: Optional files missing (e.g., BAM files IGV visualization). can continue without files, features unavailable.🔴 Red: Required files missing many files. must resolve issues proceeding. See Common Errors Solutions section help.Confirm configuration continueNote: realize Step 2 made mistake Step 1, can go back Step 1 using Back Selection button adjust configuration. didn’t make mistake notice files missing, can add directory rename needed. , use Refresh button search files .Tip: previously worked configuration saved session, can upload directly using Load Session button. allow application skip Selection Validation steps load configuration saved file.Caution: present, genes_of_interest.tsv mutation_loads.tsv files appear detected files patient, even though one file shared among patients.

heading: “Required file missing”
text: Cause: required file selected dataset type missing.Solution:Verify file exists patient directory.Check file name ensure includes patient ID (pay special attention case sensitivity).Confirm file correct extension (e.g., .tsv, .xlsx).

heading: “Multiple files found”
text: Cause: Multiple files type found single patient.Solution:Remove rename duplicate files.Ensure patient one file type.Review BAM file patterns ensure general.

heading: “Missing BAM or BAI”
text: Cause: BAM file missing corresponding index file (.bai).Solution:Generate missing .bai file using command samtools index.Ensure .bai file named correctly.example, BAM file file.bam, index file must file.bam.bai file.bai.

heading: “Missing PDF or TSV”
text: Cause: output Arriba analysis incomplete.Solution:Ensure .pdf .tsv files Arriba analysis present.Verify files include string “arriba” names correct extensions (.tsv, .pdf).FILL ALSO USE CASE EXPRESSION FILES - TISSUES FILES FILES TISSUES –> TROUBLES MATCHING TISSUE NAMES {-}

Cause: number tissues match number expression files found, tissue names matched files.Solution:Verify tissue corresponding expression file.Ensure tissue names configuration match file names (use underscores instead spaces, e.g., blood_vessel instead blood vessel).Double-check pairing tissues files validation step.

heading: Core Module Features
text: following features common across multiple analysis modules: Variant Calling, Fusion Genes, Expression Profile. shared functionalities provide consistent intuitive user experience throughout application.

heading: Interactive Data Tables
text:
data analysis modules displayed using reactable package, providing fully interactive tables real-time responsiveness.Sorting: Click column header sort data ascending descending orderBuilt-filtering: Use search boxes within column filter data --flyResizable columns: Adjust column widths dragging column bordersMulti-column operations: Combine filters sorts across multiple columns simultaneouslyTip: Hold Shift key clicking column headers sort multiple columns simultaneously.
clear sorting, hold Shift click — order cycles ascending → descending → unsorted.

heading: Filtering Menu
text:
interactive table provides basic filtering capabilities, dedicated filtering menu offers sophisticated filtering options parameters require specific logic:Custom Column Selection: Show hide columns focus relevant dataModule-Specific Filters: module provides specialized filtering options tailored data typePerformance Tip:
Apply filters reviewing marking items. Working filtered subset significantly reduces data reload times keeps application responsive.

heading: Review and Selection
text: analysis modules allow curate variants, fusions, genes intuitive selection:Mark Items: Select variants, fusions, genes directly interactive tableClinical Significance: Assign relevant clinical classifications based module typeReview Panel: Selected variants, fusions, genes appear dedicated table easy reviewEdit Selection: Remove variants, fusions, genes selection neededNote: selected variants, fusions, genes included final report. modules IGV integration, selected variants, fusions, genes can inspected IGV.

heading: Integrated Genome Viewer (IGV)
text: Validate findings read level seamless IGV integration.Multi-Patient Analysis: Select multiple patients simultaneously comparative viewingDirect Launch: Open IGV directly module pre-loaded dataRead-Level Validation: Inspect sequencing reads, coverage, alignment qualityContext Visualization: View findings genomic context annotationsThe IGV functionality relies availability BAM/BAI files. Ensure files loaded data import enable IGV data exploration.

heading: Summary and Report
text: Summary Report module provides comprehensive overview user-selected oncogenic pathogenic variants, true-positive fusions, deregulated genes across modules. module designed streamline process generating clinical reports integrating findings entire analysis workflow.

heading: Overview of Features
text: dataset type color-coded easy identification:Somatic variants: Light greenGermline variants: Dark greenFusion genes: YellowRNA expression profiles: Blue

heading: Info Boxes
text: dataset info box summarizing key metrics findings analysis, providing quick overview results. value available, displayed “N/”.

heading: Selected findings
text: section displays consolidated view flagged variants, fusions, deregulated genes selected previous modules. provides easy way review findings generating final report.Expandable boxes contain following details:

heading: Somatic and Germline Variant Calling
text: Box content: Gene name, variant type, ClinVar classification (available).Variant info: Location, Ref, Alt, HGVSc, HGVSp, Variant type.Frequency: Allelic frequency, GnomAD frequency.

heading: Fusion Genes Detection
text: Box content: Gene 1, Gene 2, Arriba confidence level.Fusion info: Gene 1, Gene 2, Position 1, Position 2, Strand 1, Strand 2.Frequency: Overall support, Arriba site.

heading: RNA Expression Profile
text: section expandable. contains summary flagged genes, including genes interest deregulated genes.

heading: Report Generation
text: Reports can generated using either predefined template user-uploaded custom template ensure report format aligns institutional regulatory requirements.

heading: Limitations
text: automatically generated report regarded draft rather final clinical document.Due limited access metadata challenge retrieving context-specific textual annotations variants, report requires manual review completion clinician.module ensures findings presented structured format suitable clinical interpretation.

heading: Creating Custom Templates
text: module supports keyword-based variable substitution populate report patient-specific findings.guidance ensures users can create custom templates tailored specific needs maintaining compatibility application’s reporting system.

heading: Variant Calling
text: Variant Calling module divided two submodules - Somatic Variant Calling Germline Variant Calling. submodules provide tools reviewing, filtering, selecting variants exploring reporting interactive tables, advanced filtering options, integrated visualizations streamline review process.information core features shared across analysis modules (interactive tables, filtering, data selection, IGV integration, data export), please refer Core Module Features chapter.

heading: Variant-Specific Filtering
text: addition core filtering capabilities, Variant Calling module provides specialized filtering options:Gene Region: Filter specifically exon splice regionsConsequence: Select multiple consequence typesCoverage Depth: Set minimum coverage thresholdsPopulation Frequency: Filter gnomAD allele frequenciesClinVar Significance: (Germline ) Filter variants clinical significanceDefault Filters Applied: first view data, following filters automatically applied ensure optimal performance focus relevant variants.• gnomAD_NFE ≤ 0.01 (rare variants )• coverage_depth > 10 (sufficient read support)• Consequence ≠ "synonymous_variant" (exclude silent mutations)• gene_region == "exon" "splice" (coding regions )

heading: Somatic Variant Calling
text: somatic submodule designed analyzing tumor-specific variants specialized metrics visualizations:Key Features:Cancer Databases: Filter COSMIC CGC_Somatic annotations

genes, biological pathways genes participate. integrative
view highlights molecular alterations connect functional contexts, offering insights

heading: Fusion Genes
text: Fusion Genes module enables users review, validate, select causal gene fusions inclusion clinical reports. module integrates supporting evidence, visualization tools, filtering options streamline review process.information core features shared across analysis modules (interactive tables, filtering, data selection, IGV integration, data export), please refer Core Module Features chapter.

heading: Fusion-Specific Table Features
text: interactive fusion table includes specialized features tailored fusion analysis:Expandable Rows: Expand rows view detailed annotations supporting evidence, including Arriba report IGV snapshot split-view mode.Visual Check Column: Use ✔️ ✖️ buttons mark fusions true-positive false-positive. helps track fusions manually reviewed.Notes Column: Add custom notes fusion, reasons marking false-positive, observations low coverage, case-specific details.Tip: Use expandable rows quickly review Arriba annotations IGV snapshots. helps identify obvious false positives, cases signal visible one gene positions.

heading: Expanded Rows
text:
expanded row functionality provides detailed view fusion, helping users quickly assess validity:Arriba Report: Displays full Arriba report selected fusion. report includes:
Fusion partners respective orientation.
Exons retained within fusion transcript.
Number supporting read counts.
snippet sequence surrounding breakpoint.

heading: Fusion-Specific Filtering
text: addition core filtering capabilities, Fusion Genes module provides specialized filtering:Tissues: Hide show specific tissue comparisonsPathways: Filter data pathway classifications

heading: Fusion-Specific IGV Integration
text: IGV integration module optimized fusion breakpoint validation:Breakpoint Visualization: Inspect fusion breakpoints tumor BAM files.Chimeric Reads: View chimeric BAM files (available) alongside tumor BAM files additional evidence.Split-View Layout: Display fusion breakpoints side side simultaneous comparison.details working chimeric reads IGV, refer Arriba Visualization Guide.Note: IGV integration module uses igv.js, web-based implementation IGV. provides essential functionalities read-level validation, may include advanced features available IGV desktop application.

heading: Expression Profile
text: Expression Profile module enables users explore analyze gene expression changes across multiple tumor sites, primary metastatic lesions, identify pathways significant deregulation based gene expression changes, include findings clinical reports.information core features shared across analysis modules (interactive tables, filtering, data selection, IGV integration, data export), please refer Core Module Features chapter.

heading: Expression-Specific Table Features
text:
Expression data displayed interactive table specialized highlighting:Color-Coded Rows: Deregulated genes highlighted based expression changes.
red -regulated genes, log2FC > 1
blue -regulated genes, log2FC < -1
orange genes p-value < 0.05
green genes p-adjustment < 0.05
red -regulated genes, log2FC > 1blue -regulated genes, log2FC < -1orange genes p-value < 0.05green genes p-adjustment < 0.05Custom Gene Lists: Upload list genes interest pathway classifications focused analysis.

heading: Expression-Specific Filtering
text: addition core filtering capabilities, Expression Profile module provides specialized filtering options:Tissues: Hide show specific tissue comparisonsPathways: Filter data pathway classificationsTissue values: Filter data based tissue types predefined buttons

heading: Volcano Plot
text:
module includes volcano plot quick visual identification deregulated genes:Log2FC vs. FDR: Genes plotted based fold change statistical significance.Highlight Genes Interest: Genes uploaded list highlighted easy identification.Note: Ensure uploaded gene list matches dataset avoid missing highlights volcano plot.

heading: Network Graph
text: Network Graph module provides interactive visualization protein-protein interactions pathway-level insights using patient-specific gene data. module leverages Cytoscape.js dynamic graph rendering integrates STRING DB interactions pathway annotations KEGG DB .

heading: Interactive Network Visualization
text:
network graph displays interactions patient genes associated pathways.Nodes: Represent genes, colored fold-change values.Edges: Represent STRING DB interactions, adjustable confidence thresholds.Dynamic Layout: Nodes edges can repositioned better visualization.Tip: Use drag--drop functionality rearrange nodes clearer view pathway interactions.two types graphs available:Main Graph
graph visualizes interactions among genes within selected pathway. can customize graph using following options located graph:
Pathway: Select pathway display interactions among genes within .
Layout Algorithms: Choose multiple layout algorithms optimize graph readability:
cola: Ideal hierarchical structures smaller graphs.
FCoSE: Best large complex networks.

heading: Customization Options
text: module allows users customize network graph:Add/Remove Nodes: Include exclude genes refine network.Confidence Thresholds: Adjust STRING DB confidence levels filter edges.Filter Evidence Type: Display edges based specific evidence types (e.g., experimental, text mining).Note: Adjusting confidence thresholds may significantly alter network structure. Use feature focus high-confidence interactions.Note: adding genes present original pathway, ensure gene names correctly formatted avoid unrecognized entries.

heading: Pathway Integration
text: module integrates KEGG pathway data contextualize gene interactions:Pathway Highlighting: Highlight nodes edges associated specific pathways.Oncogenic Pathogenic Genes: Highlight genes flagged previous modules (e.g., somatic, germline, fusion).Tip: Use pathway highlighting identify disrupted interactions within key biological processes.

heading: Tumor-Normal Comparisons
text: tumor-normal comparisons, node colors represent log2 fold-change tumor matched healthy control tissues:Upregulated Genes (fold-change > 1): Nodes colored red, intensity increasing fold-change value increases.Change (fold-change ≈ 0): Nodes colored white light gray.Downregulated Genes (fold-change < -1): Nodes colored blue, intensity increasing fold-change value becomes negative.
